grammarRule: Multipurpose word — meaning depends entirely on context
grammarRuleDetail: bitte has three main uses: (1) 'please' in requests, (2) 'you're welcome' as a response to Danke, (3) 'pardon?' or 'sorry?' when asking for repetition.
structureFormula: Bitte + request | Danke → Bitte! | Wie bitte?

grammarRule: Always singular 'gibt' regardless of what follows — takes Akkusativ
grammarRuleDetail: es gibt is a fixed impersonal expression. The verb is always gibt (3rd sg.) even when referring to plural things. The noun following always takes Akkusativ.

usage.en: Is there a supermarket here? (Akk sg.)

grammarRule: Always takes 3rd person singular verb form — same as er/sie/es
grammarRuleDetail: man is an impersonal pronoun used to make general statements without specifying who. It always triggers 3rd person singular verb forms. It cannot be used as an object — use einen (Akk) or einem (Dat).
structureFormula: Man + V(3sg) … | Das macht man nicht.

usage.en: One is not allowed to smoke here.

grammarRule: Always paired with aber or doch — zwar … aber = admittedly … but
grammarRuleDetail: zwar introduces a concession that is then countered by aber or doch. It cannot stand alone without the contrasting part. Position: after verb or at start of clause (triggers V2).
structureFormula: zwar … aber | Das ist zwar teuer, aber gut.

usage.de: Das ist zwar teuer, aber sehr gut.
usage.en: It's admittedly expensive, but very good.

usage.de: Er ist zwar müde, aber er macht weiter.
usage.en: He is tired, it's true, but he carries on.

grammarRule: Multiple uses — in negations means 'at all', in questions 'anyway', in affirmations 'in general'
grammarRuleDetail: überhaupt strengthens negations (nicht überhaupt = not at all) and questions (Warum überhaupt? = Why even?). In positive sentences it generalises.
structureFormula: gar nicht / überhaupt nicht | Warum überhaupt?

usage.en: That doesn't interest me at all.
